park_name: Discovery Parks - Fraser Street, Hervey Bay
rationale_top3:
Discovery Parks Fraser Street earns Gold status through exceptional family entertainment facilities and outstanding cleanliness standards. Families consistently praise the two heated pools, jumping pillow, games room, tennis and basketball courts, and the unique lagoon with turtles and fish that kids can feed for free. The park runs organized activities including kids club, movie nights, breakfast gatherings, and even special events like reptile shows and live music, creating a true holiday atmosphere that keeps families returning year after year.

The park's maintenance and cleanliness standards are exemplary, with multiple reviews describing it as "spotless" and "immaculate." The ensuite sites receive particular praise for their waterfall showerheads and luxury Ink & Water toiletries in 500ml bottles. Staff go above and beyond, from escorting caravans to sites and helping with parking, to checking on guests during emergencies and organizing surprise celebrations. The location just 250m from the Esplanade provides easy walking access to cafes, restaurants, and the beach, while the secure gated park with key fob access gives families peace of mind.
rationale_honourable: The beautiful natural lagoon running through the park with abundant wildlife including turtles, eels, ducks and fish provides a unique nature experience that sets this park apart. Combined with well-landscaped grounds, mature trees, and proximity to Fraser Island tours and whale watching, families enjoy both convenience and natural beauty.
water_fun: Two heated pools, one great for families
kids_play: Jumping pillow, games room, tennis, basketball, pedal carts
pet_detail: Pet friendly for powered and camping sites (not cabins), dog-friendly areas available
best_for: Families with primary school aged children who want a well-maintained park with excellent activities, wildlife experiences, and the security of a gated facility within walking distance to the beach.
executive_summary:
PARK: Discovery Parks - Fraser Street, Hervey Bay
SCORE: 82/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-22
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 174

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (17/20) — Exceptional facilities with two heated pools, jumping pillow, games room with ping pong, tennis court, basketball hoop, pedal cart hire, and unique wildlife lagoon where kids feed turtles and ducks with free food from reception. Park runs organized kids club, movie nights, breakfast gatherings, reptile shows, live music, and sausage sizzles creating genuine holiday atmosphere families love.

Nature (16/20) — Beautiful natural lagoon running through park with turtles, eels, ducks and fish provides unique wildlife experience. Abundant birdlife throughout well-landscaped grounds with mature trees. Located just 250m walk to Hervey Bay waterfront and beaches, with easy access to Fraser Island tours and whale watching. Green grassed sites and attractive gardens throughout.

Cleanliness (14/15) — Outstanding cleanliness standards with multiple reviews describing facilities as "spotless," "immaculate," and "the cleanest cabins ever stayed in." Ensuite blocks cleaned daily, pools checked multiple times daily, camp kitchens well-maintained. Staff constantly visible cleaning, maintaining grounds and facilities. Cabins prepared with AC on, music playing on arrival.

Value (13/15) — Generally good value with direct booking best, special deals like "two weeks for price of one" available. Some reviews note higher pricing compared to other parks on Queensland coast, though most feel quality justifies cost. Ensuite sites and safari tents considered excellent value for facilities provided.

Site Size (7/10) — Sites adequate but tight for larger rigs, with multiple reviews mentioning narrow access roads and tight maneuvering. Staff provide excellent assistance with parking and backing in. Ensuite sites well laid out with level pads. Some guests with big vans struggled, though assistance always available.

Sentiment (9/10) — Overwhelmingly positive with many repeat visitors over multiple years. Guests consistently praise staff friendliness and helpfulness. Families book return stays immediately on checkout. Some negative experiences with reception staff and booking issues, plus occasional construction disruption, prevent perfect score.

Location (6/10) — One street back from Esplanade means 4-5 minute walk to beach, cafes, shops, and restaurants. Easy access to Fraser Island tours and whale watching. Quiet location appreciated but not beachfront. Short drive to major supermarkets and marina.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Site access is genuinely tight for larger caravans and motorhomes with narrow roads causing difficulty for big rigs, requiring staff assistance for parking
2. Some inconsistent customer service experiences with reception staff described as "rude," "grumpy," or "miserable," plus booking and refund issues reported
3. Ongoing construction and maintenance work creates noise from grinders and chainsaws, with scaffolding blocking sites and insufficient advance notice to guests

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"best holiday parks we have stayed at on our journey," "cleanest cabins I have ever stayed in," "staff incredibly friendly and welcoming," "lagoon with heaps of turtles and fish that kids can feed," "kids club and movie nights," "spotless and immaculate ensuites," "very secure with key fob access," "walking distance to shore and beach"

rationale_top3: Exceptional cleanliness standards with amenities consistently described as 'spotlessly clean', 'immaculate', and maintained to the highest level across all facilities Outstanding coastal location within walking distance of Urangan Pier, beach, shops, restaurants, and ideal proximity for Fraser Island tours and whale watching Strong natural environment appeal with beach access, esplanade walks, sunset views, and nearby off-leash dog beach enhancing the coastal experience
rationale_honourable: Pier Caravan Park delivers exceptional cleanliness standards and a prime waterfront location near the iconic Urangan Pier, making it a solid choice for families seeking modern facilities and easy beach access. While it offers a pool, playground, and games room, the entertainment options are modest compared to resort-style parks, and the premium pricing reflects its spotless amenities rather than extensive activities—ideal for families who value pristine facilities and coastal convenience over onsite entertainment.
kids_play: Playground and games room with entertainment
pet_detail: Dog friendly with designated pet area at back of park; off-leash beach nearby.
best_for: Families with toddlers and primary school-aged children who want a clean, secure base near the beach for exploring Hervey Bay's natural attractions like Fraser Island and whale watching.
executive_summary:
PARK: Pier Caravan Park Hervey Bay
SCORE: 78/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-16
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 146

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (12/20) — Reviews mention a pool, playground for kids, and games room with ping pong and arcade games, but entertainment is modest compared to larger holiday parks. One family noted "My daughter (almost 3) and I had such an amazing time" and multiple reviews reference the games room and pool area, but there's no waterpark, jumping pillow, or extensive kids' club activities. Entertainment is adequate but not the park's main drawcard.

Nature (17/20) — Strong nature score with consistent praise for the location near Urangan Pier ("10 minutes walk"), beach access ("right next to the beach"), and proximity to Fraser Island/K'gari tours. Multiple reviews highlight "fantastic sunset views", "off leash beach nearby", and "close to the water" with esplanade walking paths. The coastal environment and access to natural attractions like whale watching are major strengths.

Cleanliness (15/15) — Outstanding cleanliness across all reviews with phrases like "spotlessly clean", "immaculate", "super clean", and "everything is kept spick and span tidy" appearing consistently. Amenities described as "immaculate regardless of what time I went" and "cleaned every day". One reviewer said "this caravan park has to be the cleanest and most organised I have ever visited."

Value (11/15) — Mixed feedback on value. Multiple reviews note "getting a little bit expensive", "$62 a night pretty exy", and "a bit on the expensive side", though most add qualifiers like "but it is worth it" and "can't complain about the quality". Free fifth night deals mentioned. Premium pricing reflects high standards but doesn't represent budget value for families.

Site Size (8/10) — Generally good site sizes with "large sites", "spacious sites", and "plenty of room" mentioned frequently. However, several reviews note challenges: "tight for average backers", "if you have a van 21ft plus, it's very very tight", and "sites are close to each other". Mix of grass and concrete slabs. Good but not exceptional space.

Sentiment (9/10) — Very positive sentiment with high repeat visitation: "Our family have agreed that it is traditional to go to the Pier once a year", "we will be back for sure", and "staying always here when in Hervey Bay". However, three 1-star reviews (April 2026) citing "rude and aggressive behaviour from staff" and security issues (bike theft) prevent a perfect score. Most recent reviews remain positive.

Location (10/10) — Perfect location score. Consistently praised as "great location", "perfect location", "walking distance to everything", "5 minute walk to the pier", close to shops, restaurants, markets, and boat harbour. Convenient for Fraser Island tours and central to Hervey Bay attractions.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Limited onsite entertainment facilities—the park has basic amenities (pool, small playground, games room) but lacks the waterparks, kids' clubs, jumping pillows, and structured activities that families with young children expect from top-tier holiday parks.
2. Premium pricing without commensurate value perception—multiple reviews note the park is "expensive" or "exy" relative to facilities offered, with costs reflecting cleanliness standards and location rather than entertainment value for families.
3. Concerning security and service incidents—several reviews document bike thefts ("lost my $2500 e-bike"), aggressive staff behaviour requiring police involvement (April 2026), and tight site access for larger vans, indicating operational inconsistencies that undermine the otherwise positive experience.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"spotlessly clean facilities", "My daughter (almost 3) and I had such an amazing time", "short walk from Urangan Pier", "games room with ping pong, arcade games", "immaculate, friendly reception", "getting a little bit expensive but it is worth it", "best park by far", "dog friendly"

rationale_top3: Cleanliness (14/15): Consistently praised across all locations with 'spotless amenities,' 'immaculate facilities,' and daily cleaning routines. New renovations at Scarness and Burrum Heads receive particular acclaim. Nature (16/20): Exceptional natural environment with multiple beachfront locations, kangaroo sightings, bird watching, proximity to Fraser Island, and stunning coastal views throughout the Fraser Coast network. Value (12/15): Mixed but generally positive value perception, with renovated locations offering 'excellent value' though some guests found premium locations overpriced compared to other parks in their travels.
rationale_honourable: Location (8/10): Strategic positioning across Fraser Coast provides convenient beach access, walking distance to shops, and proximity to Fraser Island ferry terminals. Entertainment (12/20): Basic facilities including pools and games rooms across locations, though lacking dedicated playgrounds and structured children's activities at several sites.
water_fun: Pool available at most locations with consistently clean facilities, but limited waterpark features or extensive aquatic attractions.
kids_play: Limited playground facilities across the network with some locations having no proper playgrounds. Basic games rooms with pool tables available at some sites, but minimal structured children's activities or kids clubs.
pet_detail: Pet-friendly cabins and sites available at multiple locations throughout the network. Dog-friendly beaches nearby, with some parks offering doggy day care service and welcome gifts for pets on arrival.
best_for: Beach-focused families with pets seeking clean, natural coastal settings near Fraser Island
executive_summary:
PARK: Tasman Holiday Parks - Fraser Coast
SCORE: 73/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-21
REWIEWS ANALYSED: 500

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (12/20) — Reviews indicate basic entertainment with pools, games rooms and camp kitchens across locations, but limited dedicated kids clubs or structured activities. Some locations mention "games area" and "rec room with pool table" but no waterparks or extensive children's programming. Families appreciated outdoor spaces for kids to explore but noted "no playground" at some sites.

Nature (16/20) — Strong natural environment across the network with multiple beachfront locations, "beautiful beachfront location," "right on the beach," kangaroo sightings, bird watching, and proximity to Fraser Island. Walking tracks along esplanades, "stunning views," beach access, and natural bush settings feature prominently. River Heads location offers unique rustic bush camping experience.

Cleanliness (14/15) — Overwhelmingly positive feedback on cleanliness with "spotless amenities," "immaculate facilities," "amenities cleaned every day," and "exceptionally clean" appearing frequently. New renovations at Scarness and Burrum Heads receive particular praise. Minor issues noted with some older cabins and occasional maintenance delays.

Value (12/15) — Mixed value perception with some praising "great value for money" and "excellent value" particularly at renovated locations, while others found parks "expensive," "overpriced" or "least value for money during our 6 week trip." Pricing varies significantly across the network from budget-friendly to premium rates.

Site Size (7/10) — Site sizes are inconsistent across locations with "spacious sites" and "generous grassed sites" at renovated parks contrasted with "small site," "tight sites," and "very tight for big rigs" at others. Multiple reviews mention difficulty reversing larger caravans, though staff assistance is frequently offered.

Sentiment (7/10) — Generally positive sentiment with many repeat visitors and recommendations, but significantly impacted by management inconsistencies, customer service complaints, and severe issues at specific locations (particularly River Heads midge problems). Strong positive sentiment at Burrum Heads and Scarness post-renovation, but notable complaints about staff attitude and billing issues.

Location (8/10) — Strategic locations across Fraser Coast with beach access, "walking distance to shops," proximity to Fraser Island ferries, and "central location" praised. Most parks offer easy access to Hervey Bay attractions, esplanade, restaurants and shopping. Some locations described as "15 minute walk away" from main areas.

WHY NOT 100:
Inconsistent management quality with multiple reviews citing rude staff, poor customer service, and billing disputes including double-charging complaints that remained unresolved for years. Severe sandfly and midge infestations at River Heads location made stays "unlivable" for many families despite warnings, with multiple early departures and refund requests. Limited dedicated children's entertainment facilities with no proper playgrounds at several locations, games areas "occupied by older kids," and minimal structured activities beyond basic pools and recreation rooms.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"spotless amenities," "right on the beach," "immaculate facilities," "friendly and helpful staff," "spacious sites," "pet friendly," "tight sites," "sandflies terrible," "best showers we have ever had," "rude management," "great location," "clean and well maintained," "excellent value," "overpriced," "no playground for kids"

rationale_top3: Exceptional beachfront location with direct beach access - guests consistently praise being 'right on the beach' with waves audible from sites and beautiful views at all tides Adjacent free-entry council waterpark is a huge drawcard for families, with kids loving the water slides and play facilities just steps from the park Major 2026 renovations transformed amenities to 'ridiculously clean and tidy' sparkling new condition, dramatically improving the guest experience from older 'tired' facilities
rationale_honourable: Outstanding location close to shops, restaurants and Fraser Coast attractions with easy walking/biking access along the esplanade. Recent upgrades have expanded site sizes for larger caravans. Strong repeat visitation shows family loyalty despite historical challenges.
water_fun: Adjacent council waterpark with free entry (slides require payment), direct beach access for swimming and beach play
kids_play: Next door council waterpark, nearby playgrounds, oval and walking tracks available along esplanade
pet_detail: Dog friendly in newly refurbished sections from 2026 onwards
best_for: Beach-loving families with young children seeking affordable beachfront camping with easy waterpark access and proximity to Fraser Coast attractions
executive_summary:
PARK: Pialba Beach Tourist Park
SCORE: 72/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-22
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 94

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (14/20) — Reviews consistently highlight the adjacent council waterpark as the main entertainment drawcard with families praising it as "a huge bonus for the kids" and noting kids "love it." However, the waterpark slides require payment ($8-10 for rides), and several reviews noted the park itself "lacks any guest quality guest entertainment" on-site. Playgrounds, walking tracks, and an oval are nearby, and staff made special efforts like "making Christmas special for the kids."

Nature (18/20) — Exceptional beachfront location with direct beach access is the park's standout feature. Reviews repeatedly mention "right on the beach," "10-second walk to the water," "falling asleep to waves lapping the shore," and "beautiful at both low and high tide." Great walking/biking paths along the esplanade. Some sites affected by fruit bats in fig trees, and minimal shade noted by families.

Cleanliness (12/15) — Split timeline shows dramatic improvement. Pre-2026 reviews frequently mentioned "outdated," "old and tired," "asbestos warning stickers," inconsistent hot water, and maintenance issues. Post-upgrade (2026) reviews rave about "ridiculously clean and tidy," "sparkling amenities," "brand new," and "always spotless." Transition period saw mixed experiences.

Value (12/15) — Generally positive with reviews noting "very well priced cheaper than anyone else," "$60/night services included," and "good value for what you get." However, beachfront site allocation issues caused frustration ("paid $57 just like everyone else" but got obstructed views), and some felt smaller sites meant "overpaying for what you need." Waterpark charges extra despite proximity.

Site Size (6/10) — Consistent criticism across reviews: "very small beach side spots," "sites are on the small size," "no room to park car, no room for awning," and "you'd probably have to book 2 sites" for tent plus car. Post-upgrade reviews note "new upgrade makes the sites bigger for large caravans," but space remains a constraint for many families.

Sentiment (7/10) — Strong repeat visitation evident ("5th visit," "always stay at this place," "our go to camping spot") with many "will definitely be back" comments. However, negative experiences included theft concerns, unwelcoming staff encounters, bat issues, and booking system problems. Overall sentiment improved significantly post-renovation with families not wanting to leave.

Location (9/10) — Outstanding location scores with "unbeatable beachfront location," "close to shops and restaurants," "5min drive to supermarkets," "walking distance to shopping/cafe," and "next to waterpark." Minor detractions for road noise from The Esplanade and hoon activity noted by some guests.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Limited on-site entertainment facilities for kids beyond the adjacent (paid) waterpark, with no dedicated kids club, playground equipment within the park, or organized activities that families expect from higher-tier holiday parks.
2. Site sizes remain small and cramped for family camping setups, particularly challenging for larger caravans/motorhomes with families needing space for awnings, children's play area, and vehicle parking.
3. Historical maintenance and operational issues (theft concerns, inconsistent booking system, facility breakdowns, bat problems) impacted family experiences significantly during 2023-2025 period, though major 2026 renovations addressed many concerns.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"waterpark nearby was a huge bonus for the kids," "right on the beach," "amenities ridiculously clean and tidy," "very small beach side spots," "perfect for families," "kids had so much fun," "falling asleep to the sound of waves," "great location on the beach and next to waterpark"

park_name: Fraser Coast Beachfront Tourist Parks | Scarness
rationale_top3: Exceptional beachfront location with direct beach access, calm swimming conditions, stunning ocean views, and walking distance to shops, restaurants, and Fraser Island tour pickups Immaculate modern amenities following major renovation, with individual ensuite-style bathrooms described as spotless and cleaned daily, plus well-maintained camp kitchen Strong natural environment score with sheltered north-facing beach, dolphin sightings from sites, beautiful sunsets, and peaceful morning bird sounds
rationale_honourable: Fraser Coast Beachfront Tourist Parks at Scarness delivers an exceptional beachfront location with direct beach access, modern renovated amenities, and walking distance to shops and restaurants. While some families mention traffic noise from the esplanade and limited on-site entertainment for kids, the park's stunning ocean views, clean facilities, and proximity to Fraser Island tours make it a solid choice for families seeking a relaxed coastal holiday with natural beach play.
water_fun: Beach swimming directly at park, no pool
kids_play: Beach play and exploring, no playground mentioned
pet_detail: Pets not allowed based on review evidence
best_for: This park is best for families with young children who love beach play and swimming in calm waters, and who value location and cleanliness over on-site kids entertainment facilities
executive_summary:
PARK: Fraser Coast Beachfront Tourist Parks | Scarness
SCORE: 72/100
DATE SCORED: 2025-05-17
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 190

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (8/20) — Reviews consistently note the park has no playground, pool, spa, or organized kids activities. Entertainment comes from the beach itself, with families mentioning swimming, beach walks, dolphin spotting, and proximity to bike paths and the esplanade. One review mentioned "pool, spa" but this appears to be an outlier or confusion with another park, as the vast majority make no mention of water facilities beyond the ocean.

Nature (18/20) — Exceptional natural environment with direct beach access described as "absolute beachfront," "right on the water," and "steps from the beach." Multiple reviews mention calm, safe swimming conditions, stunning sunsets, morning bird sounds, dolphins visible from sites, and beautiful ocean views. The north-facing Scarness Beach location is sheltered and peaceful, with Fraser Island blocking wind.

Cleanliness (14/15) — Overwhelmingly positive feedback on cleanliness with recent reviews praising "immaculate," "spotless," "pristine" amenities following major renovation. Individual ensuite-style bathroom facilities with shower, toilet, and sink are described as "modern," "exceptional," and "cleaned daily." Camp kitchen also receives consistent praise for cleanliness. Minor negative notes about occasional maintenance lapses under relief management and older reviews pre-renovation.

Value (12/15) — Mixed sentiment on value with many families stating it's "worth it" and "excellent price for powered sites" at approximately $56-66 per night for beachfront. Several reviews note it's "higher than others but worth it" for the location. Some disappointment expressed about paying premium prices for beachfront sites with limited views or when issues arose. Overall considered fair value for a prime beachfront council park.

Site Size (7/10) — Reviews indicate "generous," "quite large," and "spacious sites" post-renovation with concrete slabs, grassed areas, and room for large vans and awnings. However, multiple families note sites can be "tight," "packed like sardines" during peak times, with some struggling to park cars and caravans. Sites against the fence near the road are described as notably smaller. Management often helps with backing in, suggesting space can be challenging.

Sentiment (8/10) — Strong repeat visitation with many families returning annually for years or decades ("30 years," "every year," "2nd visit," "3rd revisit"). High recommendation rate and emotional attachment ("second home," "love coming back"). However, sentiment is significantly impacted by inconsistent management experiences, with some families reporting rude treatment, overzealous rule enforcement, or poor customer service that "ruined" holidays. Recent reviews under managers Jess & Darren are overwhelmingly positive.

Location (10/10) — Perfect location score with universal praise for being "centrally located on Esplanade," "walking distance to everything," "shops across the road," and proximity to restaurants, cafes, pub, grocery store, and Fraser Island tour pickups. The beachfront position is described as ideal, with no roads to cross to access the beach and beautiful bike/walking paths along the foreshore.

WHY NOT 100:
1. **No on-site kids entertainment facilities** — The park has no playground, jumping pillow, pool, games room, or organized activities, which significantly limits appeal for families with young children who need structured entertainment beyond beach play.

2. **Significant traffic and road noise** — Multiple reviews warn of "constant traffic noise at all hours," "hoons with loud exhausts," particularly Friday/Saturday nights, and the need for "earplugs." Sites near the road are notably affected, and the park sits between the esplanade and beach, making some noise unavoidable.

3. **Inconsistent management and customer service** — While recent managers receive glowing praise, several families report poor experiences with previous management including rudeness, intimidation, overzealous rules, poor communication about site bookings, and unwelcoming attitudes that left families feeling "treated like intruders" rather than valued guests.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"right on the beach," "direct beach access," "amenities are immaculate," "Jess & Darren are absolute legends," "some road noise," "bring your earplugs and padlocks," "no playground or pool," "walking distance to everything," "generous site space post-renovation," "calm ocean swimming," "dolphins playing in front of us," "higher price but worth it," "traffic noise at all hours," "beachfront views are stunning"

park_name: Fraser Coast Beachfront Tourist Parks | Burrum Heads
rationale_top3: Outstanding beachfront location with direct beach access, calm ocean swimming perfect for young children, beautiful sunsets, and dolphin sightings earning 18/20 for nature Exceptional cleanliness standards with consistently spotless and immaculate amenities, grounds maintained meticulously by highly-praised management teams earning 14/15 Strong value proposition despite premium pricing, with beachfront sites offering excellent coastal holiday experience and recent renovations elevating facility quality earning 12/15
rationale_honourable: Fraser Coast Beachfront Tourist Parks at Burrum Heads earns a Silver rating for its exceptional beachfront location and recently renovated facilities that families consistently praise. The park's immaculate cleanliness, helpful management teams (Sean, Kym, Nigel, and Fiona repeatedly mentioned), and direct beach access create a relaxing coastal holiday experience, though the absence of dedicated kids' entertainment facilities and tight site configurations prevent it from reaching Gold status for young families.
water_fun: Beach swimming, calm ocean, no pool onsite
kids_play: No playground, beach access only for play
pet_detail: Pet-friendly sites available in southern section of park (dog-friendly area mentioned)
best_for: Families with younger children who love beach activities, fishing, and a relaxed coastal atmosphere without needing organized kids' clubs or waterparks
executive_summary:
PARK: Fraser Coast Beachfront Tourist Parks | Burrum Heads
SCORE: 72/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-21
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 190

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (8/20) — No onsite kids entertainment, playground, or pool facilities mentioned across all reviews; families relied entirely on beach access and natural play opportunities with no structured activities or kids' clubs available.

Nature (18/20) — Outstanding beachfront location with direct beach access, calm ocean swimming perfect for young children, beautiful sunsets, dolphin sightings, pleasant river walks at Burrum Heads location, and sheltered beach environment; "absolute beachfront," "right on the water," and "wake up to the waves" repeatedly praised.

Cleanliness (14/15) — Consistently described as "spotless," "immaculate," "exceptionally clean" throughout recent reviews; management teams (Sean & Kym, Nigel & Fiona, Justin & Megan) praised for meticulous daily cleaning of amenities, grounds, and facilities; recent renovations elevated cleanliness standards significantly.

Value (12/15) — Mixed feedback with some noting "pricier" rates ($70/night mentioned) but most agreed "you get what you pay for" with quality facilities; beachfront sites at $56/night considered excellent value; some concern about paying full price during construction periods.

Site Size (6/10) — Significant concerns about tight sites, especially pre-renovation; "packed like sardines," "sites are quite small," "very hard to get van into" frequently mentioned; newer renovated areas have "quite large" and "spacious sites" but older sections remain cramped with difficult backing access.

Sentiment (9/10) — Overwhelmingly positive repeat visitation with families returning annually for 16-30+ years; management teams consistently praised as "legends," "wonderful," "fantastic"; strong loyalty despite some site allocation frustrations and construction disruptions.

Location (5/10) — This score reflects the park's ACCESSIBILITY rather than beach position (which is covered in Nature). Located at Burrum Heads/Torquay on Fraser Coast, not in a major hub; "away from the busy part of Hervey Bay" noted; shops, bakery, butcher across road convenient but limited overall town facilities; good for Fraser Island day trips but not centrally located for broader family attractions.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Complete absence of kids' entertainment facilities (no playground, pool, waterpark, or kids' club) limits appeal for families wanting structured activities beyond beach play.
2. Site size issues persist in older sections with tight, cramped spaces difficult for larger rigs, limited car parking, and challenging reversing despite helpful management assistance.
3. Road noise from esplanade traffic with "hoons racing" at night, some sites only 2-3 metres from footpath/road, and construction noise during ongoing renovations detract from the peaceful experience.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"absolute beachfront," "amenities are spotless," "immaculate amenities & camp kitchen," "managers are absolute legends," "sites are quite tight," "packed like sardines," "recently renovated," "right on the water," "calm ocean every day," "no playground or pool," "shops across the road," "very helpful staff"

park_name: Fraser Coast Beachfront Tourist Parks | Torquay
rationale_top3: Outstanding beachfront location with direct beach access, dolphin sightings, safe swimming in calm ocean waters, and extensive walking/bike tracks along the esplanade consistently praised by families Consistently clean and well-maintained facilities described as 'spotless' and 'cleaned daily' with management praised for maintaining high standards throughout the park Excellent location convenience with walking distance to beach, shops, cafes and restaurants, Foodworks across the road, and easy access for Fraser Island day trips
rationale_honourable: This beachfront park delivers outstanding natural beauty with direct beach access and dolphin sightings, consistently praised for its absolute waterfront position and clean facilities. While it lacks dedicated kids' entertainment and sites can be tight, families seeking a relaxed beach holiday with walking, swimming, and easy access to shops will find excellent value in this no-frills coastal gem.
water_fun: Pool and spa mentioned; beachfront swimming
kids_play: Beach activities; zooper doopers distributed occasionally
best_for: Families seeking a relaxed, beach-focused holiday with young children who enjoy natural coastal activities, safe swimming, and convenient access to local shops and cafes
executive_summary:
PARK: Fraser Coast Beachfront Tourist Parks | Torquay
SCORE: 68/100
DATE SCORED: 2025-01-10
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 106

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (8/20) — Limited organized kids' activities with occasional treats like zooper doopers mentioned; main entertainment is beach-based play, swimming, and walking/bike tracks along esplanade. One mention of pool and spa but not consistently referenced. No dedicated playground or waterpark facilities noted.

Nature (18/20) — Outstanding beachfront location with direct beach access described as "absolute beachfront" and "right on the water"; families report dolphin sightings, safe swimming in calm ocean, beautiful sunsets, and extensive walking/bike tracks along esplanade. Natural environment consistently praised.

Cleanliness (13/15) — Facilities repeatedly described as "very clean," "spotless," and "cleaned daily" with well-maintained amenities, though some reviews note older amenities and occasional issues with washers. Management praised for maintaining high standards.

Value (12/15) — Positive mentions of "excellent price for powered sites" and "$56 a night" for oceanfront views. Most families report good value, though some felt small sites weren't worth the price. Generally competitive for beachfront location.

Site Size (6/10) — Mixed feedback: some describe sites as "quite large" post-rebuild with room for large vans, while others report being "packed in like sardines" with "small sites no room to park your car." Tight sites especially near fence; beachfront rows appear more spacious.

Sentiment (8/10) — Strong repeat visitation with families returning yearly for 30+ years in some cases. Mostly positive sentiment about managers and staff being helpful and friendly. Some negative experiences with booking errors and customer service.

Location (9/10) — Excellent location praised as "walking distance to beach and shops," "close to cafes restaurants," with Foodworks across road and pub nearby. Convenient for Fraser Island day trips. Some road noise from esplanade reported.

WHY NOT 100:
1. No dedicated kids' entertainment facilities or playground—families rely entirely on beach activities with minimal organized entertainment beyond occasional treats from managers.
2. Site size inconsistency creates cramped conditions especially in back rows near fence, with multiple reports of difficulty maneuvering vans and lack of car parking space.
3. Road noise from esplanade hoons and traffic impacts some sites, particularly those away from beachfront, affecting the peaceful atmosphere several families expected.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"Kids loved having the beach straight outside their camper", "dolphins playing right in front of us", "absolute beachfront", "packed in like sardines", "amenities spotless", "walking distance to shops", "sites are quite large", "zooper doopers for the kids", "excellent price for powered sites", "safe swimming"

rationale_top3: Strong cleanliness standards with maximum score reflecting well-maintained facilities Good entertainment and nature amenities both achieving maximum scores Solid value for money proposition with above-average score
rationale_honourable: Reasonable location score indicates convenient access to Hervey Bay attractions and Fraser Coast region
water_fun: Standard holiday park aquatic facilities available
kids_play: Entertainment facilities provided with good activity options for children
pet_detail: Pets accepted with moderate restrictions - recommend confirming pet policies before booking

rationale_top3: Excellent quiet location within walking distance to Point Vernon beach and dog-friendly off-leash areas, easy 5-10 minute drive to Hervey Bay town center, whale watching, and Fraser Island tours. Friendly and accommodating staff who frequently go above and beyond, organizing community events like free sausage sizzles, Easter egg hunts, and scavenger hunts that create a welcoming family atmosphere. Popular magnesium pool that guests consistently praise as a highlight, providing a unique swimming experience in a relaxed setting away from the main Hervey Bay hustle.
rationale_honourable: Despite significant challenges with older facilities and ongoing renovations, the park maintains a loyal following due to its peaceful residential setting, community-oriented atmosphere, and staff who genuinely care about guest experience. The proximity to dog beaches and walking paths makes it particularly appealing for pet owners seeking a quieter base for exploring the Fraser Coast.
water_fun: Magnesium pool popular, no pool shade
kids_play: Basic playground, games room often closed/dirty
pet_detail: Pet friendly with dog beach nearby; dogs must stay on-site in cabin or tied up; some permanent residents enforce strict pet rules
best_for: Pet owners seeking dog-friendly beach access, families planning day trips who need an affordable base, and guests who value staff friendliness over modern amenities
executive_summary:
PARK: BIG4 Hervey Bay Holiday Park
SCORE: 58/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-15
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 273

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (8/20) — Reviews consistently mention limited on-site entertainment with a basic games room that was often closed, dirty, or relocated to a hot tin shed during renovations; pool table and equipment frequently non-functional; however, staff organized free sausage sizzles, Easter egg hunts, and scavenger hunts that families appreciated.

Nature (12/20) — Park is located in a residential area but within walking distance (2-5 minutes) to Point Vernon beach and dog-friendly off-leash areas; beach is rocky with limited sand; nice bike paths and esplanade walks nearby; some trees and birdlife on-site but not a heavily natural setting.

Cleanliness (9/15) — Mixed reviews with many praising clean amenities and staff efforts, but significant complaints about dirty cabins with expired milk, mouldy showers, grout buildup, hair in drains, lack of basic supplies, and floors so dirty feet turned black; amenities described as old but "always kept clean" by some, "dirty" and "inadequate" by others.

Value (8/15) — Reviews cite "dynamic pricing" charging up to $85-95/night for powered sites despite advertised rates from $63; many felt overpriced for the dated facilities and lack of entertainment; some appreciated "cheap rates" and "excellent value" especially given the location, but majority felt it didn't match typical Big4 standards.

Site Size (5/10) — Frequent complaints about "very small" and cramped sites, narrow roads making reversing difficult, sites "too close to each other" where neighbours could be heard talking; staff helped with parking but multiple reviews describe sites as inadequate for modern caravans; some permanents have junk around sites.

Sentiment (6/10) — Polarized reviews with enthusiastic repeat visitors praising staff friendliness and community atmosphere, but substantial negative sentiment from families who left early, felt deceived by online photos, encountered rude permanent residents or staff, and would "never stay there again"; many recommend alternative parks in Hervey Bay.

Location (10/10) — Strong consensus that location is excellent: quiet residential area away from main Hervey Bay hustle, short walk to beach and convenience store, easy 5-10 minute drive to town center, shops, restaurants, whale watching, and Fraser Island tours; dog beach and esplanade walks highly praised.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Extensive negative feedback about run-down, dirty cabins and amenities that don't meet Big4 standards, with guests reporting expired food, mouldy bathrooms, inadequate cleaning, broken equipment, and floors so dirty their feet turned black.
2. Severely limited on-site entertainment and facilities for children, with games room often closed/relocated during renovations, broken pool tables, no fans in summer, no pool lights for evening swimming, and nothing to keep kids occupied on-site requiring constant day trips.
3. Very small, cramped caravan sites with narrow roads that make parking difficult even with staff assistance, permanent residents creating a "trailer park vibe," and ongoing construction disruption with no advance warning to guests about renovation noise and mess.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"most horrendous camping experience we have ever had", "nothing for the kids to do", "very run down park", "cabin floor was disgusting", "staff are amazing", "magnesium pool was a highlight", "away from the hustle and bustle", "not a modern Big 4"

rationale_top3: Severe seasonal sandfly and midge infestation renders the park "unlivable" for many families during warmer months, with multiple families forced to leave early despite trying multiple repellents, causing sleep deprivation and extreme discomfort especially for young children. Inconsistent and reportedly hostile customer service with owners described as "rude and unfriendly," "dismissive," hanging up on guests, refusing refunds for midge-related early departures, and giving impression guests are "bothering her." No on-site kids entertainment facilities (no playground, pool, or activities), limited operational hours (strict 2pm check-in, owner unavailable after 5pm), no dump point for caravans, and gate access issues reported by multiple guests.
rationale_honourable: Fraser Coast RV Park offers an authentic bush camping experience with excellent natural surroundings, kangaroos, and beautifully designed rustic facilities that families appreciate. The spacious sites, creative amenities, and proximity to Fraser Island make it a unique base for nature-loving families, though significant insect issues and inconsistent customer service experiences prevent it from reaching higher classification.
water_fun: No pool or water features mentioned
kids_play: Fairy house, exploring acres, wildlife watching
pet_detail: Pet friendly with resident dog Rosie; doggy day care service available while visiting Fraser Island
best_for: Adventurous families with young children who enjoy exploring nature, wildlife spotting (kangaroos, frogs, birds), and want an authentic bush camping experience as a base for Fraser Island trips during cooler months.
executive_summary:
PARK: Fraser Coast RV Park
SCORE: 58/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-08
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 70

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (8/20) — Limited structured kids entertainment; families appreciated the fairy house, exploring acres of land, watching kangaroos and frogs, and occasional visits from Rosie the dog, but no playground, pool, or organized activities mentioned.
Nature (18/20) — Excellent bush setting with kangaroos, birds, frogs, shaded sites, walking trails, and proximity to Fraser Island ferry (3-4 minutes); families loved the natural environment and wildlife viewing opportunities.
Cleanliness (13/15) — Facilities consistently described as "spotless," "immaculate," and "super clean" with creative rustic design; innovative showers highly praised but some maintenance issues noted (gate malfunctions, kitchen light issues).
Value (11/15) — Mixed feedback; some found it "budget friendly" at $39/night with good value, while others felt it expensive for limited amenities, especially given severe insect problems and unexpected storage fees.
Site Size (9/10) — Spacious sites with "heaps of room," "plenty of space to spread out," and "not jammed in like a Big4"; powered sites accommodate 4 vans around central pole with extension cables provided.
Sentiment (5/10) — Highly polarized reviews; many 5-star experiences offset by multiple 1-star reviews citing severe midge infestations forcing early departures and poor customer service responses to complaints.
Location (8/10) — Excellent location for Fraser Island access (right near ferry departure), 20 minutes from Hervey Bay, but isolated bush setting with no nearby beaches or attractions beyond nature-based activities.

WHY NOT 100:
[1] Severe seasonal sandfly and midge infestation renders the park "unlivable" for many families during warmer months, with multiple families forced to leave early despite trying multiple repellents, causing sleep deprivation and extreme discomfort especially for young children.
[2] Inconsistent and reportedly hostile customer service with owners described as "rude and unfriendly," "dismissive," hanging up on guests, refusing refunds for midge-related early departures, and giving impression guests are "bothering her."
[3] No on-site kids entertainment facilities (no playground, pool, or activities), limited operational hours (strict 2pm check-in, owner unavailable after 5pm), no dump point for caravans, and gate access issues reported by multiple guests.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"kids absolutely loved the fairy house," "the best showers possibly EVER," "midges are terrible. Worst I have experienced," "woke to the van infested in midges," "rude and dismissive response," "lots of space for the kids to run," "kangaroos graising in the mornings," "doggy day care while we did a Fraser Island day trip"

rationale_top3: Severe cleanliness inconsistencies with multiple reports of filthy cabins, bed bugs, dog hair on bedding, broken fixtures, and poorly maintained caravan sites with rubbish and safety hazards. Site sizes are critically tight and unsuitable for larger family rigs, with difficult access, congestion, and multiple warnings about space limitations for caravans over 26ft. Limited kids entertainment infrastructure with only a basic rec room and no dedicated playground, waterpark features, or structured activities for young families.
rationale_honourable: Fraser Coast Holiday Park offers an excellent beachfront location just 50m from the water, with friendly management that goes above and beyond for guests. The pool and spa facilities receive consistent praise, and the park is genuinely pet-friendly with secure gates. While the park shows promise with recent upgrades and enthusiastic staff, inconsistent cleanliness standards and very tight sites hold it back from higher achievement.
kids_play: Rec room with pool table, arcade, games
pet_detail: Dog-friendly with pet cabins available and secure gates at night
best_for: Families with pets seeking an affordable beachside park are best suited here, especially those traveling with smaller vans or staying in cabins.
executive_summary:
PARK: Fraser Coast Holiday Park
SCORE: 56/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-04-30
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 58

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (10/20) — Limited on-site kids entertainment with a rec room featuring pool table, arcade machine, board games and TV mentioned positively, but no dedicated playground or structured activities for young children evident in reviews.

Nature (14/20) — Excellent beach proximity at just 50m from the esplanade with multiple reviews praising beach access for walks and fishing, though limited natural bush or creek environment on-site beyond grassy sites with some nice trees.

Cleanliness (8/15) — Highly inconsistent with some praising "very spacious and clean" cabins and "spotless" amenities, while numerous recent reviews report "dog hair all over blankets," "filthy couch," "bed bugs," "gritty floors," safety hazards, and sites "strewn with rubbish."

Value (9/15) — Mixed feedback with some finding it "great value" and "cheap and not nasty," but others reporting $160/night for dog-friendly cabins, "huge variations in prices" ($25 vs $59 for similar sites), and concerns about pricing inconsistencies between quote and actual charge.

Site Size (4/10) — Consistently criticized as "little tight for big rigs," "parking very tight," "extremely small and tight," "small sites" suitable only for small vans, with congestion and difficult access frequently mentioned.

Sentiment (5/10) — Polarized with loyal repeat visitors praising management ("Natalie is amazing"), but significant negative experiences including management disputes, noise complaints, medical episodes from guest conflicts, and strong warnings to "avoid this park."

Location (9/10) — Outstanding location praised universally as "walking distance to beach," "close to shops and cafes," "100m walk to esplanade," "close to everything," and "centrally located" in Hervey Bay.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Severe cleanliness inconsistencies with multiple reports of filthy cabins, bed bugs, dog hair on bedding, broken fixtures, and poorly maintained caravan sites with rubbish and safety hazards.
2. Site sizes are critically tight and unsuitable for larger family rigs, with difficult access, congestion, and multiple warnings about space limitations for caravans over 26ft.
3. Limited kids entertainment infrastructure with only a basic rec room and no dedicated playground, waterpark features, or structured activities for young families.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"Only 50m to the beach," "pool and spa awesome," "little tight for big rigs," "dog hair all over the blankets," "bed bugs," "parking onsite is extremely small and tight," "The managers are absolute legends," "Facilities need repairs"
